bins.name: Expedited interconnection pathways
bins.desc: OpenAI supports multiple expedited interconnection mechanisms: DOE designation of nationally-important projects with required transmission provider fast-tracking, and optional curtailment-based fast-track pathways where flexible loads receive interconnection priority over inflexible ones.
quotes: Consistent with Principle 4, nationally-important projects designated by DOE should be expedited.

OpenAI supports an approach that allows the DOE to identify and provide a national-interest designation for loads above 250 MW that have clear national interest or security, economic imperative or competitiveness, or critical-infrastructure value.
Transmission providers should then be required to expedite the interconnection of these designated projects through a fast track process.
OpenAI supports an optional curtailable-load pathway where full or partial curtailment obligations of the large loads allow the transmission provider to expedite the interconnection request.
Large loads that agree to curtail demand when called on by system operators for a predetermined number of hours should be given interconnection priority over inflexible loads.

bins.name: Curtailment flexibility benefits
bins.desc: Flexible, curtailable loads reduce transmission system impacts, enable better capacity utilization, and enhance system efficiency by distinguishing ready loads from inflexible ones.
quotes: This will distinguish ready, flexible loads with reduced impacts on the electric transmission system and enable available transmission capacity to be put to beneficial use, thereby enhancing system efficiency.
Large loads that agree to curtail demand when called on by system operators for a predetermined number of hours should be given interconnection priority over inflexible loads.

bins.name: Streamlined interconnection studies
bins.desc: Net-impact studies for hybrid facilities should replace normal queue processes to capture real system effects and reduce study burdens and duration, and standardized readiness criteria help transmission providers prioritize cost-effective and viable projects.
quotes: Consistent with Principle 4, nationally-important projects designated by DOE should be expedited.
Transmission providers should then be required to expedite the interconnection of these designated projects through a fast track process.
OpenAI supports a clear pathway for load paired with on-site or behind-the-meter generation to be evaluated on a net-impact basis to capture real system effects and reduce study burdens and duration.
This will allow transmission providers to prioritize and expedite the most rational, cost-effective, and grid-complimentary projects.

OpenAI supports standardized readiness criteria that classify large loads based on strict metrics for commercial readiness, end user alignment, site and engineering maturity, and near-term operational timelines.
This will help all parties to maximize efficiency by directing scarce resources towards vetted, viable projects.

bins.name: Cost-effective project prioritization
bins.desc: Readiness standards and net-impact evaluation help maximize efficiency by directing scarce resources toward vetted, viable, and cost-effective projects rather than speculative ones.
quotes: This will allow transmission providers to prioritize and expedite the most rational, cost-effective, and grid-complimentary projects.
This will help all parties to maximize efficiency by directing scarce resources towards vetted, viable projects.

OpenAI supports allowing, subject to appropriate protections, commercially-ready large loads to fund and voluntarily construct required network upgrades identified by the transmission provider, when doing so will accelerate the interconnection.

bins.name: Hybrid facility net-impact pathway
bins.desc: Load paired with on-site or behind-the-meter generation should be evaluated on a net-impact basis outside the normal queue process, and hybrid loads are particularly suited to fund network upgrades to accelerate their own interconnection.
quotes: OpenAI supports a clear pathway for load paired with on-site or behind-the-meter generation to be evaluated on a net-impact basis to capture real system effects and reduce study burdens and duration.
This approach could be particularly appropriate for large loads paired with on-site or hybrid generation.

bins.name: Load-funded network upgrades
bins.desc: Commercially-ready large loads should be allowed, subject to appropriate protections, to fund and voluntarily construct required network upgrades when doing so will accelerate interconnection, particularly for hybrid facilities.
quotes: OpenAI supports allowing, subject to appropriate protections, commercially-ready large loads to fund and voluntarily construct required network upgrades identified by the transmission provider, when doing so will accelerate the interconnection.
This approach could be particularly appropriate for large loads paired with on-site or hybrid generation.

bins.name: Withdrawal penalty differentiation
bins.desc: OpenAI supports reduced withdrawal penalties for ready projects with valid end-user agreements that withdraw due to external factors, while maintaining full penalties for speculative loads that lack end-user offtake agreements.
quotes: For large load projects that meet the readiness criteria and have secured valid end user agreements but subsequently withdraw for reasons beyond their control, withdrawal penalties should be reduced.
Speculative loads that withdraw without end user offtake agreements should remain subject to full withdrawal penalties.

bins.name: Transmission system capacity utilization
bins.desc: Flexible loads enable better utilization of available transmission capacity and reduce impacts on the electric transmission system.
quotes: This will distinguish ready, flexible loads with reduced impacts on the electric transmission system and enable available transmission capacity to be put to beneficial use, thereby enhancing system efficiency.
